remarks.name: Peter Steinberger
remarks.role: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world.
remarks.text:
It's both amazing and painful to watch codex use browser + computer use to open Chrome, go to my PR, tap on comment and wrangle with the macOS picker - all TO UPLOAD AN IMAGE. GitHub has no API doesn't stop anyone.

I let my codex run in VMs so they don't steal app focus. https://t.co/Dd5KzNs6E5
remarks.translation: 看着Codex用浏览器+电脑操作打开Chrome，进入我的PR，点击评论，再跟macOS的文件选择器较劲——就为了上传一张图片，真是既神奇又让人心疼。GitHub没有API也拦不住谁。我让Codex在虚拟机里运行，这样它们就不会抢走应用焦点。https://t.co/Dd5KzNs6E5

remarks.role: Claude is an AI assistant built by anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8dz3D or download the app.
remarks.text:
We know this has been frustrating, and we want to give you more certainty about what your plan includes. We are making access standard at 50% usage for the plans that use Fable most intensively.

Thank you for your patience over the past several weeks. We're continuing to invest in new capacity and will keep everyone updated as we do.
remarks.translation: 我们知道这令人沮丧，我们希望能让您更清楚地了解您的计划包含的内容。对于使用Fable最频繁的计划，我们将访问权限标准设定为50%的使用率。感谢您过去几周的耐心等待。我们将持续投资于新的容量，并在过程中随时向大家通报进展。

alpha only gone when we all stop asking these level of questions and start discussion about on-policy autoaeo (does claude optimizing your aeo disproportionately work on claude?) vs generalizable aeo

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
The reason I want this btw is spending an entire day staring at screens trying to manage agents fries my brain.

Much better to be walking around outside talking to the agents "on the phone" giving them work to do and having them update me on status via voice.

Can't wait for the first lab to ship this.
remarks.translation: 顺便说一句，我想要这个的原因是整天盯着屏幕管理智能体让我脑子发麻。相比之下，在外面边走边用“电话”跟智能体沟通，给他们分配任务，让他们通过语音汇报进度，这样舒服多了。真希望第一个实验室能尽快推出这个功能。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
Why do you think kimi hurts Google?

Many enterprises won’t consume Kimi directly. They’ll get it through Google Cloud because they still need enterprise guarantees - security, data residency, compliance and most importantly, chips.

Money out one pocket into the other.
remarks.translation: 为什么你认为Kimi会伤害谷歌？许多企业不会直接使用Kimi。他们会通过谷歌云获取，因为他们仍然需要企业级保障——安全性、数据驻留、合规性，以及最重要的，芯片。不过是左手倒右手罢了。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
This post is key. The cheaper AI gets, the more opportunity there is for the entire ecosystem  - especially including end-customers - to benefit.

Everything is bottlenecked by being able to successfully and cost effectively deploy AI in real workloads. Any time we can lower the cost of AI, the total usage goes up. When that happens, the value accrues to all layers of the stack, as noted.

The only modification is I think as AI gets more efficient, demand even for the frontier closed models can go up too. You often need the strongest model possible for the orchestration of a task, then you can farm out work to cheaper or more tuned models for the bulk of tokens. 

This efficiency gain can ironically lead to even more frontier spend in the process because you can cost effectively deploy AI against more tasks. The thing at most risk is margins, which is fair, and in general it seems that intelligence eventually should converge with the margins of the infrastructure stack.

Anyway, super fun times to see competition play out to drive down the cost of AI.
remarks.translation: 这篇帖子很关键。AI成本越低，整个生态系统——尤其是终端客户——获益的机会就越大。一切瓶颈都在于能否成功且经济高效地将AI部署到实际工作负载中。每当AI成本降低，总使用量就会上升。正如所述，价值会因此惠及技术栈的各个层面。唯一需要补充的是，我认为随着AI效率提升，对前沿闭源模型的需求也可能增加。通常需要最强大的模型来统筹任务，然后将大量token的处理工作分派给更便宜或更专用的模型。这种效率提升反而可能带来更多前沿模型支出，因为你可以经济高效地将AI部署到更多任务中。风险最大的是利润率，这很合理，而且总体来看，智能最终似乎会与基础设施栈的利润率趋同。总之，见证竞争推动AI成本下降，真是令人兴奋的时代。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
The reason enterprises struggle to go beyond basic chat bots is the talent gap to build harnesses and evals.

1. Evals: do you clearly understand your use cases and can you replicate that in the form of offline and online evals. Do the evals express your ambition and do they push the jagged frontier of the models? Do they help you pick the right models on the quality-cost-latency curve? 

2. Harness: do you have a system that manages routing, multiagency orchestration, context management, tool calling, memory that is independent of the models?

3. Talent: do you have the talent to build this all out on the frontier? 
This is the scarcest piece.
remarks.translation: 企业难以超越基础聊天机器人的原因，在于构建驾驭系统和评估体系的人才缺口。1. 评估体系：你是否清晰理解自身用例，并能将其转化为离线与在线评估？这些评估是否体现了你的雄心，能否推动模型突破现有能力边界？它们能否帮助你在质量、成本、延迟的平衡曲线上选择合适模型？2. 驾驭系统：你是否拥有独立于模型之外的管理系统，能处理路由、多智能体协调、上下文管理、工具调用和记忆功能？3. 人才储备：你是否具备在前沿领域构建这一切的人才？这是最稀缺的环节。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text:
Tip for building in public:

If making content feels like extra work, show the work already happening inside your product. A tiny screen recording, the first version, or the user behavior that changed your design. The reasoning matters more than production value.
remarks.translation: 公开构建的提示：如果制作内容感觉像是额外的工作，那就展示产品内部已经进行的工作。一段小小的屏幕录制、第一个版本，或是改变了你设计的用户行为。背后的思考比制作价值更重要。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text:
Just a few years ago, many people were uncomfortable with recording meetings 

Now it’s just assumed that all business meetings are recorded (not for humans, but for agents)

Technology really changes culture
remarks.translation: 就在几年前，很多人还对会议录音感到不自在。如今，所有商务会议默认都会被录下来（不是为了给人听，而是给智能体用）。技术确实在改变文化。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.text: Spot birds not golf

Google

podcasts.title: 为什么前沿生态系统必须开放——Matei Zaharia 与 Reynold Xin，Databricks
podcasts.originalTitle: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
podcasts.excerpt: 我们非常高兴地欢迎Databricks参加AIEWF，与AI工程师生态系统中数百家顶尖公司齐聚一堂。LS订阅用户可使用专属折扣，在晚鸟价基础上再享优惠，并获得价值超过$50k的赞助商礼遇！大家仍在热议Satya的前沿生态系统文章，但真正基于此构建实践的却寥寥无几（<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…
podcasts.originalExcerpt: We’re excited to have Databricks join us at AIEWF, among hundreds of the top companies in the AI Engineer ecosystem. LS subscribers can use their discount to get past the late bird pricing and access over $50k in sponsor offers! Everyone is still talking about Satya’s Frontier Ecosystems post, but few have actually built a (<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: 构建一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
podcasts.originalTitle: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
podcasts.excerpt: 如果规模定律成立——Surge AI 首席执行官埃德温·陈相信它们确实成立——我们正飞速冲向一个未来，在那里，人类能做的任何事，AI 都能做得更好。当 OpenAI 的模型运用新颖的代数几何技术，推翻了数学家保罗·埃尔德什提出的一个公开猜想时，菲尔兹奖得主蒂莫西·高尔斯敏锐地感受到了这种转变。他起初以为模型证明了一个上界，并做好了心理准备：那将意味着“数学家很快就要完蛋了”。当他意识到它只是找到了一个反例时，他松了口气——这为他争取了一两年时间，让他毕生投入的事业不至于那么快就被 AI 超越。作为为各大模型公司提供训练模型所用的数据环境和评估系统的公司创始人和首席执行官，陈对 AI 模型吸收那些我们曾视为人类专属任务的速度，有着独特的视角……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: If scaling laws hold—and Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen believes they do—we’re hurtling toward a future where there’s nothing humans can do that AI can’t do better. When OpenAI’s models disproved an open conjecture posed by mathematician Paul Erdős using novel algebraic geometry techniques, Fields medalist Timothy Gowers felt the shift acutely. He initially thought the model had proved an upper bound, and braced himself: that would mean it was “all over for mathematicians very soon.” When he realized it had only found a counterexample, he was relieved—it bought him another year or two before the thing he’s devoted his life to becomes something AI does better.As founder and CEO of the company behind the data environments and evals the major model companies use to train their models, Chen has a unique perspective on how quickly AI models are absorbing tasks we used to think of as uniqu…
